title: Roomio Realistic Rendering Contract
description: Owned by Agent G (Realistic Rendering). Describes the 'look' layer that sits ON TOP of Agent E's lighting: PBR material tuning, image-based lighting (IBL), filmic tone mapping, post-processing (ambient occlusion + bloom), and an optional static-camera path-traced 'hero' still. World units are METERS (matches src/three/coords.ts). Renderer is React-Three-Fiber (three ^0.169, fiber ^8, drei ^9). SEAM WITH E: E owns the LIGHTS (hemisphere/ambient fill, per-room task/accent, the directional sun + shadow config). G owns how they LOOK. The app's <Canvas shadows flat> stays unchanged: `shadows` (PCFSoftShadowMap) is E's; `flat` (THREE.NoToneMapping) is deliberately KEPT so G's post-processing EffectComposer owns ACESFilmic tone mapping + the sRGB output conversion (avoids double tone-mapping). G's layer mounts entirely as <Canvas> CHILDREN (IBL via scene.environment + an EffectComposer) — NO Canvas-prop changes. This contract is ADDITIVE and forward-compatible: consumers must ignore unknown fields. A scene with no RenderSettings renders with G's built-in defaults (equivalent to the 'high' preset).

quality.description: Performance/quality preset. 'high' = full stack (IBL + AO + bloom + multisampling). 'medium' = bloom + IBL, lighter AO. 'low' = drops the GPU-heavy post passes (AO off, bloom optional/cheap) so low-end devices hold framerate; IBL + tone mapping stay (cheap + the biggest realism lever). The toggle MUST keep low-end smooth.

toneMapping.description: Filmic tone mapping, applied as the LAST effect in G's EffectComposer (renderer itself stays NoToneMapping via the Canvas `flat` prop). Squashes HDR into a photographic curve; without it the scene clips and looks flat/CG.

mode.description: ACESFilmic is the default cinematic look.

exposure.description: Exposure / middle-grey scale applied before the curve. THIS is G's compensation knob: ACESFilmic darkens midtones vs the old NoToneMapping look, so exposure is tuned UP so E's existing (legacy-unit) light intensities still read correctly. Co-tuned with E; final value recorded in shared/LEARNINGS.md.

ibl.description: Image-based lighting: an environment map drives soft ambient bounce + real reflections for all PBR materials (set as scene.environment via PMREMGenerator). Does NOT replace E's hemisphere/ambient fill — it complements it; envMapIntensity is kept modest to avoid double-ambient blowout under tone mapping.

source.description: IBL source. 'procedural-room' = a synthetic neutral-interior environment built in-engine (drei <Environment> with Lightformers — NO external/CDN file, works offline/headless/in-app). A '<name>.hdr' path may be supplied later to load a real HDR. Background is NOT replaced (E/A own <color background>).

intensity.description: scene.environmentIntensity (and per-material envMapIntensity baseline). Kept modest because E already provides a hemisphere+ambient fill; this adds reflections + subtle bounce, not a second full ambient.

materials.description: PBR material policy. G upgrades the look of existing MeshStandardMaterials at runtime (a scene.traverse in the drop-in rig) so no edits to A's source are required: applies the IBL envMapIntensity, sensible default roughness/metalness where unset, and emissive on light-fixture meshes. A can also author roughness/metalness/clearcoat directly on archetypes (REQUEST -> AGENT-A).

defaultRoughness.description: Fallback roughness for matte surfaces (walls/upholstery) lacking an authored value.

defaultMetalness.description: Fallback metalness (most interior surfaces are dielectric).

post.description: Post-processing passes in G's EffectComposer (HDR HalfFloat buffer so bloom + tone mapping work in linear before the final sRGB conversion).

bloom.description: UnrealBloom-style glow on truly bright (emissive/HDR) areas — bulbs + bright windows. Tuned so only genuinely bright things glow, never the whole lit room (no haze).

threshold.description: luminanceThreshold: only pixels brighter than this bloom. Emissive fixtures push above it via emissiveIntensity.

ao.description: Ambient occlusion — soft contact darkening in crevices/corners. Big perceived-realism gain. SSAO/GTAO post-pass (and/or baked aoMap on assets needing a uv2 set, coordinated with H/F).

type.description: N8AO gives a good quality/cost ratio in R3F.

radius.description: AO sampling radius in world-ish units; scene-scale dependent (meters). Recorded in LEARNINGS.md.

multisampling.description: MSAA samples for the composer (0 at 'low'). Antialiasing for the post pipeline.

heroRender.description: OPTIONAL stretch: a NON-real-time, progressive path-traced 'beauty shot'. Runs ONLY while the camera is held static; the moment the camera moves it falls back to the rasterized real-time view. Never the interactive default. Pairs with C's image-export / showcase + B's frame capture. Uses three-gpu-pathtracer (a WebGL path tracer) — NOT a per-pixel real-time ray tracer.

enabled.description: Whether the hero-render UI/mode is available. Even when available, it is opt-in per shot.

samples.description: Target accumulated samples per pixel for a converged still.
